items.title: Outgoing chief magistrate on mistakes, rehabilitation and compassion

children.content: Heart is not something anyone expects from the court system, but it was something outgoing ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker, who retired in March after 15 years in the job, aimed to portray.

items.title: Truck driver found guilty of negligent driving over fatal crash in Canberra

children.content: Sean Joshua Walton was driving his truck and turning right at an intersection when he crashed into a black Harley Davidson motorbike being driven by 47-year-old Craig O'Neil.

image.altText: A crumpled motorbike on the ground near a parked truck, at night at an intersection.

items.title: Just one rental in Australia affordable for JobSeekers like Jess

children.content: Anglicare Australia's latest look at rentals shows the private market effectively does not exist for poorer Australians.

items.title: 'Obvious I lied': Former Canberra doctor faces disciplinary action for lying under oath

items.title: 'Breach of trust': Woman convicted of illegally obtaining medication

children.content: A Canberra speech pathologist is convicted of using the details of people she knew, including parents of two of her patients, to illegally obtain prescription medication.

items.title: Hopes new Canberra strata recommendations will dispel 'shonks' from industry

children.content: An ACT Legislative Assembly committee inquiry wants to see strata managers get mandatory licenses.

items.title: Knife used in Canberra Hospital incident 'secreted' from ACT police in earlier search

children.content: The ACT's chief police officer says the knife produced by a Canberra Hospital patient that led to an hours-long security incident had not been detected by police during a search of the man earlier in the day.

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

A trio of young Aboriginal boys climb out of a public pool.

items.title: Australian 'ISIS brides' in limbo after reportedly being blocked from reaching Syrian airport

children.content: A week since a group of 13 Australian women and children with ties to former Islamic State fighters left a Syrian refugee camp for Australia, they remain in the capital Damascus in a state of limbo.

items.title: 'Gentle parenting' under fire from this alternative school's supporters

children.content: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

items.title: Banksy behind overnight reveal of new statue in central London

items.title: Queensland mining company fined $7m after death of 'beloved Poppy'
